cards.prompt: Memory<T> can be a field of a class (stored on the managed heap), unlike Span<T> which was CS8345
cards.code: using System; class Holder { public Memory<int> field; } var h = new Holder(); h.field = new int[10]; Console.WriteLine($"memoryAsClassField={h.field.Length==10}");

cards.prompt: Memory<T>.Span conversion exists but the reverse Span<T>-to-Memory<T> does not (Rule #1 rationale)
cards.code: using System; using System.Linq; using System.Reflection; bool memHasSpan = typeof(Memory<int>).GetProperty("Span") != null; bool spanHasAsMemory = typeof(Span<int>).GetMethods().Any(m => m.Name.Contains("Memory")); Console.WriteLine($"memoryHasSpanProp={memHasSpan} spanHasToMemory={spanHasAsMemory}");

cards.prompt: MemoryPool<T>.Rent returns an IMemoryOwner<T> (IDisposable) that the caller owns and must Dispose (Rule #7)
cards.code: using System; using System.Buffers; IMemoryOwner<int> owner = MemoryPool<int>.Shared.Rent(100); bool isOwner = owner is IDisposable; int len = owner.Memory.Length; owner.Dispose(); Console.WriteLine($"rentGivesDisposableOwner={isOwner} lenAtLeast100={len>=100}");
